compute.cost_note: CPU-bound; two reused stdlib audits (stance B4, agency B3) plus regex AGD markers per document; no model load

do_not_use_when: paragraph-role / discourse-mode labeling is needed (that is the B1/B2 LLM judge, a separate surface)
a provenance verdict about a text or person is wanted (no verdict by design)

inputs.target: a corpus directory of .txt/.md documents (default substantiality floor 300 words; --compare takes two directories)

artifacts: compare JSON (per-signal Cliff's delta + Cohen's d, sorted by |delta|)

purpose: aggregate the deterministic B4 stance + B3 abstraction signals plus the net-new AGD marker module (assuring/guarding/discounting) over a corpus into an argument-move population profile, and run the two-corpus separation test (Cliff's delta primary, Cohen's d secondary) — the cheap, judge-free half of ArgScope, gating any B1/B2 LLM-judge spend.
use_when: building an argument-register population baseline (the profiles the acquisition-tier registers feed)
answering whether deterministic signals separate two corpora (e.g. human vs LLM argument) before spending on the LLM judge
